front.title: AI Ethics in Fiction
front.subtitle: Exploring the moral boundaries and social responsibilities of artificial minds through narrative.

back.text:
Science fiction serves as a primary laboratory for testing the ethical implications of artificial intelligence long before the technology becomes reality. Authors use speculative narratives to explore themes of machine personhood, the rights of sentient software, and the potential for algorithmic bias. These stories challenge audiences to consider whether a sufficiently advanced AI deserves legal protections or if it remains a mere tool of its creators.

One of the most influential frameworks in this field is Isaac Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics, which attempted to hard-code morality into machine logic. While these laws were designed to prevent harm, Asimov's stories often focused on the logical loopholes and unforeseen consequences that arise when rigid rules meet complex real-world situations. This highlights a central challenge in modern AI safety: the difficulty of aligning machine goals with nuanced human values.
